The development comes amid wider political churn in Kerala’s alliance politics, where local adjustments often signal deeper negotiations between partners. By reopening space for KC(M), the Congress appears to be seeking a practical accommodation that could ease tensions within the coalition arrangement. The hospital committee issue has attracted attention because it reflects the balance of influence among alliance partners in district-level institutions. The move is being watched closely by political observers who see it as part of the continuing effort to manage competing interests within the state’s coalition framework.

ticker_headline: Tamil Nadu to honour filmmaker Bhagyaraj with state honours
blog_title: State to accord honours to filmmaker Bhagyaraj after his passing
blog_content: Tamil Nadu will accord state honours to filmmaker Bhagyaraj after his death, according to the Chief Minister’s announcement. Bhagyaraj was a respected figure in Tamil cinema, known for his work as a writer, director and actor, and his passing has prompted tributes from across the film industry. The decision to provide state honours reflects the government’s recognition of his cultural contribution and his long-standing influence on Tamil cinema. Public figures and fans have expressed condolences, remembering his popular films and distinctive storytelling style. The announcement is expected to be followed by official ceremonial arrangements as the state pays tribute to one of its well-known creative voices.

ticker_headline: Man arrested in ₹100-crore cheating case involving private schools
blog_title: Police arrest accused in major fraud case targeting Tamil Nadu private schools
blog_content: Police have arrested a man accused of cheating private schools across Tamil Nadu of more than ₹100 crore. Investigators say the accused allegedly operated an unregistered organisation under the name of a private schools body and used it to deceive institutions. The scale of the alleged fraud has drawn attention because it involved a large network of schools and significant sums of money. Authorities are now expected to trace the flow of funds and identify whether more people were involved in the operation. The case has also raised concern among education administrators and school managements, who are likely to demand tighter verification mechanisms to prevent similar scams in the future.
social_post: A major fraud case involving private schools in Tamil Nadu has led to an arrest, with alleged losses crossing ₹100 crore. Police are probing the wider network. #TamilNadu #Fraud #Schools #PoliceAction
